A fourth text with the identical gap was recently identified. Theologians have debated for centuries whether the Faerûnian Pantheon's missing member was deliberately excised from the historical record, was never canonised, or represents something the records were never permitted to describe accurately. The Silent Synod's file on this question is their oldest open case. It is also the case they discuss least frequently in written correspondence.

title: The Seldarine — The Elven Court
label: Pantheons
excerpt: A court rather than a coalition — bound by kinship, shared history, and the Long Memory tradition that preserves theological truth across timescales no human institution survives intact. The Seldarine's divine cold war strategy is patience itself: elves remember what mortal civilisations forget, and in Aethrya, memory is the most dangerous weapon of all.
text: Pantheons The Seldarine — The Elven Court A court rather than a coalition — bound by kinship, shared history, and the Long Memory tradition that preserves theological truth across timescales no human institution survives intact. The Seldarine's divine cold war strategy is patience itself: elves remember what mortal civilisations forget, and in Aethrya, memory is the most dangerous weapon of all. Corellon Larethian — The First Among Equals God of Arts, Magic, and Elven Identity · Protector of the Preservation Circles Arts Magic Elves Pre-Veil Knowledge Corellon's Preservation Circles in Silvenmoor maintain libraries of pre-Veil knowledge — documentation of what the world looked like before gods walked in disguise. Four thousand years of careful divine archival management have not erased these records. The Circles know things three pantheons would prefer they didn't. Their continued, uninterrupted operation is Corellon's most defiant act in the current era, and one that several gods have repeatedly considered "addressing" before concluding that addressing it would confirm the very concerns the Circles have been documenting. Mortal Form: An ancient Sun Elf archivist known as the Keeper, whose documented tenure in the Silvenmoor preservation complex extends back six hundred years without any record of aging. The Sun Elves regard this as evidence of exceptional divine blessing. It is, technically, accurate. Aerdrie Faenya — The Winged Mother Goddess of Air, Wind, and the Avariel Air Wind Avians Long-Distance Communication Aerdrie Faenya's Windrunner network is Aethrya's most effective long-distance intelligence service — Avariel carrying information between separated communities with a speed ground-bound networks cannot match. The Windrunners are simultaneously a divine intelligence operation and a genuine humanitarian aid organisation. Aerdrie has spent four thousand years concluding this combination is the most efficient possible use of the same infrastructure. Rillifane Rallathil — The Leaflord God of Nature and Druids · Architect of the Grove Network Nature Druids Wild Spaces Ecological Memory The Grove Network is Rillifane's living instrument — a distributed system of Druid circles connected through shared ecological monitoring that Circle-Speaker Verdant coordinates across the continent's wild spaces. The contamination pattern that Verdant is documenting was first identified in Rillifane's own divine perception approximately two centuries ago. He has been waiting for the right mortal — or the right collection of mortals — to identify it from below. He is still waiting. The pattern is almost complete enough to be undeniable. Erevan Ilesere — The Chameleon God of Mischief and Change · The Trickster of the Seldarine Mischief Change Information Erevan's mortal form as the street performer Merry Tanglewood is Aethrya's most accessible divine interaction point for Bard characters — and its most carefully calibrated one. Merry operates at a level of social and performance capability that is mechanically equivalent to a Master Bard with College of Eloquence. Merry has been shaping what stories get told, to whom, and in what form, for longer than any mortal tradition can document. What Merry is interested in hearing, right now, from the characters who share their performance space, is a question the DM team considers the most open-ended active prompt in the server's current story architecture.

title: The Dark Seldarine — The Drow Pantheon
label: Pantheons
excerpt: The divine assembly governing the Sundered — the Drow who chose or were driven into the deep places. Unlike the Faerûnian Pantheon, which manages mortal ignorance of divine presence, the Dark Seldarine governs a civilisation where divine manipulation is not a hidden background assumption but an acknowledged, expected, and celebrated element of daily political life.
text: Pantheons The Dark Seldarine — The Drow Pantheon The divine assembly governing the Sundered — the Drow who chose or were driven into the deep places. Unlike the Faerûnian Pantheon, which manages mortal ignorance of divine presence, the Dark Seldarine governs a civilisation where divine manipulation is not a hidden background assumption but an acknowledged, expected, and celebrated element of daily political life. Maerath — The Spider Queen Goddess of Spiders, Secrets, and Drow Society · The Infinite Web Spiders Secrets Drow Chaos Intelligence Operations Maerath's mortal intelligence network is the largest and most sophisticated divine operation active in Aethrya. Her web reaches from the deepest Underdark passages to Leilon's merchant quarter — from Southkrypt's trading houses to the Children's surface cells in Emberreach. No single point in the network can trace the full shape of what it belongs to. The Veilbreakers' file on Maerath is marked "Scope: Unknown" and has remained at that classification for thirty years, because every attempt to establish scope has produced only more questions. Maerath is the only deity in Aethrya who approaches the Veil Accord's disguise requirement as a structural advantage rather than an imposed constraint. Her operation functions more effectively when no mortal can identify who is coordinating it. The Accord, for Maerath, is optimal working conditions. Mortal Forms: Multiple simultaneous identities maintained across different regions and social strata. The Children's inner leadership knows one. Velkharra's matriarchal houses know another. No entity who knows one knows both. This arrangement is entirely intentional. Vhaeraun — The Masked Lord God of Thievery and Surface-Seeking Drow · The Male Voice of the Sundered Thievery Surface Drow Male Drow Autonomy Vhaeraun's cult in Southkrypt provides financial networks, escape assistance, and the particular satisfaction of dismantling matriarchal authority from within. His conflict with Maerath is theologically irreconcilable and personally vicious — making Southkrypt's Drow community the setting's most internally volatile factional environment, where two divine networks operate in the same spaces with mutual awareness and mutual contempt. Eilistraee — The Dark Maiden Goddess of Beauty, Dance, and Redemption · The Silver Shore Beauty Song Dance Drow Seeking the Surface Eilistraee represents the possibility that the Sundering was not a permanent theological verdict — that Drow who choose to walk toward light choose something real rather than abandoning something essential. Her mortal network operates near the surface, providing genuine community infrastructure for Drow seeking surface life: not merely the financial autonomy Vhaeraun offers, but belonging itself. In a world as fractured as Aethrya's, belonging is among the most revolutionary things any divine network can offer.

title: The Morndinsamman — The Dwarven Pantheon
label: Pantheons
excerpt: The divine family of the dwarven peoples — whose forge-temple institutions have shaped dwarven civilisation for longer than the Veil Accord has existed, whose divine presences in the Irondeep are the most institutionally embedded of any pantheon in Aethrya, and whose relationship to the Primordial Titan awakening carries theological implications none of them have fully addressed aloud.
text: Pantheons The Morndinsamman — The Dwarven Pantheon The divine family of the dwarven peoples — whose forge-temple institutions have shaped dwarven civilisation for longer than the Veil Accord has existed, whose divine presences in the Irondeep are the most institutionally embedded of any pantheon in Aethrya, and whose relationship to the Primordial Titan awakening carries theological implications none of them have fully addressed aloud. Moradin — The Soul Forger Father of Dwarves · God of Creation, Smithing, and Dwarven Identity Creation Smithing Dwarves Mountain Holds Moradin's forge-temples are simultaneously religious centres, craft guilds, and the primary social institutions around which dwarven clan life organises. The forge-blessing ceremony that every dwarven smith performs at the start of each working day is, in Aethrya's theological reality, a direct divine-to-mortal power transmission that sustains a warm channel without constituting the miraculous intervention the Accord prohibits. The line between tradition and active divine presence, in dwarven culture, has been intentionally blurred for four thousand years. Moradin considers this his finest administrative achievement. Mortal Form: Durn Anvilhand , master smith and forge-temple overseer in the Irondeep's primary Mountain Dwarf hold. His work is flawless. It has been demonstrably flawless for two hundred consecutive years. The dwarves regard this as evidence of exceptional divine favour. It is evidence of something considerably more direct. Vergadain — The Laughing Dwarf God of Wealth, Trade, and the Bright Bargain Wealth Luck Commerce Cross-Race Trade Vergadain's mortal form as the traveling merchant Goldan Brightpurse is Aethrya's most mobile divine operation — present at every significant trade event in the Irondeep and increasingly at cross-race commercial gatherings in the border regions. Goldan's network of trade contacts represents the most comprehensive economic intelligence map of the Irondeep's commercial relationships held by any entity in Aethrya. He attributes his encyclopaedic commercial memory to "excellent record-keeping." He is, genuinely, not wrong about this. Mortal Form: Goldan Brightpurse , traveling merchant, who has attended every major trade fair in the Irondeep for three centuries under various names and whose commercial relationships span every significant clan and guild in the region. Dumathoin — Keeper of Secrets Under the Mountain God of Mining, Buried Wealth, and the Deep Places' Silence Mining Mountains Buried Secrets What the Stone Keeps Dumathoin's domain is what the mountain retains — buried wealth, concealed veins, and the things placed in deep darkness that were intended to remain there. His relationship to the Primordial Titan awakening is one of the most significant theological tensions in the current season: the Titans are buried things. Dumathoin's portfolio technically includes jurisdiction over what occurs when buried things of sufficient scale begin to stir. He has not yet publicly addressed what that means in practice. The silence itself is, by several accounts, informative.

title: Yondalla's Children — The Halfling Pantheon
label: Pantheons
excerpt: The divine family of the halfling peoples — whose mortal strategy of quiet protectiveness, institutional invisibility, and deliberate cultivation of being underestimated has produced results that would astonish every faction that has ever moved against a halfling community and found the response more coordinated than the attacker anticipated.
text: Pantheons Yondalla's Children — The Halfling Pantheon The divine family of the halfling peoples — whose mortal strategy of quiet protectiveness, institutional invisibility, and deliberate cultivation of being underestimated has produced results that would astonish every faction that has ever moved against a halfling community and found the response more coordinated than the attacker anticipated. Yondalla — The Protector and Provider Mother of Halflings · Goddess of Protection, Fertility, and the Quiet Network Halflings Protection Community Accumulated Goodwill Yondalla's divine cold war strategy is the most genuinely counterintuitive in Aethrya: do not accumulate power — accumulate goodwill. Four thousand years of halfling communities providing hospitality, counsel, and quiet competence to their neighbours has produced a network of genuine obligations that Yondalla can activate when necessary. The faction that threatens a halfling community discovers, too late, that three other factions have longstanding positive relationships with that community and will act to protect it — not from political allegiance, but because halflings have spent a very long time being authentically helpful to people who remember it. Mortal Form: Bria Goodhearth , halfling innkeeper in Leilon, whose establishment has served as the genuine heart of four surrounding districts for thirty years. Every major faction in Leilon has at least one figure who owes Bria Goodhearth a meaningful favour. This is not coincidence. It is the most patient intelligence operation in the city, and the only one that functions through genuine affection rather than leverage. Arvoreen — The Defender God of Halfling Warriors · The Quick Blade Behind the Warm Hearth Halfling Defence Vigilance The Cost of Underestimation Arvoreen is the tactical edge of Yondalla's protective network — the quiet guarantee that being underestimated carries a cost. Halfling communities with active Arvoreen shrine traditions are defensively organised in ways that consistently surprise attackers who accounted for size and failed to account for preparation. His mortal form is a halfling guard captain whose response times and tactical assessments are described by every colleague as "impossibly good" — an assessment that is both entirely accurate and not quite explanatory enough.

title: Lords of the Golden Hills — The Gnomish Pantheon
label: Pantheons
excerpt: The divine family whose engineering, wit, and foundational theological curiosity have produced the season's most strategically significant technological development: a device that detects divine presence. Operational. In mortal hands. Functional at forty feet. Currently being extended. Every deity in Aethrya has an opinion about this. None has formally acted.
text: Pantheons Lords of the Golden Hills — The Gnomish Pantheon The divine family whose engineering, wit, and foundational theological curiosity have produced the season's most strategically significant technological development: a device that detects divine presence. Operational. In mortal hands. Functional at forty feet. Currently being extended. Every deity in Aethrya has an opinion about this. None has formally acted. Garl Glittergold — The Watchful Protector Father of Gnomes · God of Wit, Gemcutting, and Holy Mischief Gnomes Wit Gemcutting Illusory Arts Divine Detection Garl Glittergold's mortal persona of Galdrick Sparkwit is the most quietly audacious divine operation in Aethrya's current story arc. Galdrick's divine-presence detection device has been under development in Millhaven's gnomish engineering district for approximately eight years, presented publicly as a "theological curiosity detector" and demonstrated to visitors with the infectious enthusiasm of someone who has not fully considered its implications. Galdrick has fully considered its implications. The device is functional, the range is being extended, and every divine faction in Aethrya has silently noted that no one has formally moved to stop it — because the first deity to do so establishes that stopping it was necessary, which is precisely the confirmation the Veilbreakers have been seeking for thirty years. Mortal Form: Galdrick Sparkwit , Millhaven inventor. His detection device is currently the most strategically consequential object in Aethrya. He presents it with delight. The delight is genuine. Flandal Steelskin — Master of Metal God of Gnomish Smithing, Fire, and Masterwork Fabrication Smithing Fire Mining Precision Engineering Flandal's mortal form as Flan Ironpocket is the fabrication expert who built the detection device's most technically demanding components. Flan's workshop produces the most precisely engineered small-scale metalwork in the region, attracting commissions from clients who cannot easily explain how they knew Flan was precisely the right person for a specific piece of work. Flan and Galdrick have been collaborating for forty years. Each suspects what the other is. Neither has yet confirmed it. The working relationship has produced the most significant technological artifact in Aethrya regardless, which both of them consider an advertisement for collaboration over certainty. Mortal Form: Flan Ironpocket , Millhaven fabricator. The components Flan builds for Galdrick's device are the work of four thousand years of divine engineering knowledge expressed through the hands of a gnome who takes professional pride in not explaining how they achieved the tolerances they did.

title: The Primordial Titans — Before the Gods
label: Pantheons
excerpt: The ancient presences that predate every pantheon, every accord, every theological tradition in the known world — not gods in any recognisable sense, not bound by any covenant, and currently expressing themselves through geological events that four data points in the Grove Network's contamination map are beginning to make terrifyingly legible.
text: Pantheons The Primordial Titans — Before the Gods The ancient presences that predate every pantheon, every accord, every theological tradition in the known world — not gods in any recognisable sense, not bound by any covenant, and currently expressing themselves through geological events that four data points in the Grove Network's contamination map are beginning to make terrifyingly legible. "The Veil Accord governs divine beings who operate within the mortal plane in ways mortals would classify as miraculous. The Primordial Titans do not operate. They exist. The Accord does not address what occurs when existence at that scale begins to express itself through the shape of the stone itself." — Silent Synod research paper, highest classification, two of three copies currently unaccounted for. Overview The Primordial Titans are the oldest divine presences in Aethrya — entities that predate the Faerûnian Pantheon's formation by millennia, predate the Seldarine's court structure, predate every organised theological tradition in the known world. They are not gods in any recognisable framework: they do not maintain mortal institutions, do not seek worship, and do not participate in the divine cold war's political architecture. They simply are — enormous, ancient, patient, and currently moving in ways that geological stability does not explain and that the gods watching from their mortal forms have not finished arguing about how to address. The Veil Accord does not address the Primordial Titans. Whether this is because Ao determined they were outside the Accord's scope, because the gods negotiating could not agree what language to use for entities who operate at a scale that makes language feel approximate, or because acknowledging them in the Accord's text would require acknowledging what their existence implies about the nature of divinity itself — this remains the Silent Synod's most significant open research question, and the one they are most careful to discuss only in person. The Tide-That-Remembers The oceanic Primordial — associated with the deep sea and the things that have inhabited it since before mortal civilisation began naming things. Deep Sashelas's aquatic research operations have been documenting unusual oceanic behaviour patterns for sixty years. The patterns have been accelerating for three. The Waking One The terrestrial Primordial — whose proximity produces the contamination pattern the Grove Network is documenting. Its awakening is not a single event but a gradual, centuries-long increase in geological and ecological expression that Circle-Speaker Verdant's four data points are beginning to define. The shape forming in the data is not geological. It is intentional. The Seventh Referenced in the Silent Synod's oldest documents and in Primordial Titan-era texts held by Corellon's Preservation Circles. Not confirmed as currently active. The oldest records describe the Seventh's domain in terms that resist translation into any language currently in use — which is either a limitation of ancient translation or a property of the domain itself. The Synod has not reached consensus on which. The Primordial Titan awakening is Aethrya's season-defining escalating pressure — present in Phandalin's seismic reports, in the Grove Network's contamination data, in the Zealot Barbarian's investigation thread, in the geological anomalies Rangers encounter beyond the frontier. When the pattern is identified and its shape is fully understood, what follows will not be an ending. It will be the beginning of something the divine cold war's current participants have no framework to address together. The gods are not ready. The mortals are certainly not ready. The characters that Aethrya's players have built together will decide, in the space between those two certainties, what happens when ready stops being an option.

title: Spells Overview
label: Spells & Rituals
excerpt: Aethrya's full spell system — organised by tier from Cantrip to Master, with a ritual system that connects magical practice to divine institutional networks in ways that even experienced spellcasters may not have fully accounted for.
text: Spells & Rituals Spells Overview Aethrya's full spell system — organised by tier from Cantrip to Master, with a ritual system that connects magical practice to divine institutional networks in ways that even experienced spellcasters may not have fully accounted for. All spells in Aethrya use the Spell Point system. Spell tiers correspond to mastery levels — Novice spells for Novice-tier characters, up to Master spells for Master-tier characters. Each tier requires the prerequisite mastery to be achieved before the spell is accessible. No mechanical workaround exists for this requirement; it is a narrative gate reflecting the genuine growth required to responsibly wield each level of magical capability. Cantrips & Fluff Magic Zero-cost magical expressions — available to all spellcasters from character creation. In Aethrya, even a Prestidigitation carries the signature of Mystra's Weave. Nothing is truly unremarkable. Novice Tier (1–2 SP) Foundational spells — Cure Wounds, Magic Missile, Charm Person, Detect Magic. The building blocks of arcane and divine practice, and the spells that constitute the visible surface of magic as most mortals understand it. Adept Tier (2–3 SP) Meaningful capability — Counterspell, Fireball, Dispel Magic, Haste. At this tier, magical choices begin drawing the attention of divine monitoring networks in ways that Novice casting rarely does. Expert Tier (4–5 SP) World-shaping potential — Teleportation Circle, Scrying, True Seeing, Wall of Force. Each casting at this tier is a statement about the caster's place in Aethrya's power structure. Master Tier (5–7 SP) Legendary capability — Wish, Simulacrum, Meteor Swarm, Astral Projection. Master-tier casting is geopolitically significant. You are, at this level, impossible to ignore. Rituals Extended magical workings that cost time rather than SP — and whose completion updates divine monitoring networks, Grove maps, and institutional records in ways that immediate casting does not.

title: Novice Tier Spells
label: Spells & Rituals
excerpt: The foundational threads of magical practice — 1st and 2nd level spells that represent a spellcaster's first deliberate, controlled engagement with Mystra's living Weave. Every cantrip is an unremarkable thread pulled from a goddess's framework. In Aethrya, even the unremarkable is observed.
text: Spells & Rituals Novice Tier Spells The foundational threads of magical practice — 1st and 2nd level spells that represent a spellcaster's first deliberate, controlled engagement with Mystra's living Weave. Every cantrip is an unremarkable thread pulled from a goddess's framework. In Aethrya, even the unremarkable is observed. Cure Wounds Evocation Novice Cleric · Druid · Paladin · Ranger · Bard Touch-range healing — 1d8 + spellcasting modifier, scaling with upcasting. The Harvest Fellowship's primary field medicine spell and the most frequently cast magic in Aethrya by a significant margin. In Maren Seedhallow's hands, a routine casting. Everywhere else, still remarkable. Magic Missile Evocation Novice Wizard · Sorcerer Three darts of magical force that cannot miss. The Church of the Seven Stars uses this spell as every new arcane student's first controlled casting exercise — it demonstrates the Weave's reliable availability more cleanly than any other. Tessara Wand's records show which students cast it first try without flinching. She finds that predictive. Charm Person Enchantment Novice All Casters The target regards you as a friendly acquaintance for the duration. Subject to the full Charm and Dominate Rules consent framework. Velara Dusk's counselling practice almost certainly deploys this spell in ways the consent framework was specifically designed to address. Almost certainly. Detect Magic Divination Novice (Ritual) Most Casters Sense magical auras within 30 feet for 10 minutes. Notably: Detect Magic cannot identify divine disguises, because they are not Weave-mediated magical effects in the standard sense. This limitation is documented in the Silent Synod's research as one of the primary empirical distinctions between divine and arcane magic — a distinction most theological curricula have not incorporated. Misty Step Conjuration Novice Wizard · Sorcerer · Warlock · Paladin Teleport up to 30 feet as a bonus action, surrounded by a brief silvery mist. The most commonly flagged spell in Veilbreaker Field Division evidence files — its signature mist is distinctive and recognisable. Three documented incidents in the last year matched Misty Step signatures to known operatives. Two of those operatives are now in someone else's files. Healing Word Evocation Novice Cleric · Druid · Bard Bonus action healing at range — less potent than Cure Wounds, vastly more tactically flexible. The spell that first demonstrates to newer Clerics why divine support differs from battlefield medicine: the ability to sustain healing without surrendering action economy is the foundation of effective divine care in Aethrya's complex combat landscape. Thunderwave Evocation Novice Wizard · Druid · Bard · Sorcerer A wave of thunderous force sweeps from the caster, pushing creatures and objects. One of the few combat spells that leaves unambiguous physical evidence of its use — shattered objects, displaced dust, a distinctive acoustic signature recorded in multiple witness accounts. The Hub Guard maintains a list of suspected regular casters based entirely on incident scene reconstruction. Identify Divination Novice (Ritual) Wizard · Artificer · Bard Learn a magical item's properties and command words, or discern active spells on a creature. Critically: Identify reveals spell school, not divine origin. It cannot determine whether a magical effect was initiated by a hidden god. This limitation, documented in the Silent Synod's research, is not an accident of the spell's design.

title: Adept Tier Spells
label: Spells & Rituals
excerpt: Third and fourth level spells — where the Weave's capabilities begin asserting themselves in ways that draw divine attention more consistently. The tier at which most experienced spellcasters spend the majority of their active careers, and the tier at which the consequences of magical choices become genuinely difficult to contain.
text: Spells & Rituals Adept Tier Spells Third and fourth level spells — where the Weave's capabilities begin asserting themselves in ways that draw divine attention more consistently. The tier at which most experienced spellcasters spend the majority of their active careers, and the tier at which the consequences of magical choices become genuinely difficult to contain. Counterspell Abjuration Adept Wizard · Sorcerer · Warlock Interrupt and cancel a spell as it is cast. The tactical pivot point in any encounter involving multiple spellcasters. In divine-political terms, a Counterspell that successfully interrupts what turns out to be a divine agent's working creates a documentation trail — Veilbreaker Field Division operatives are specifically trained to recognise the signature of Counterspell interactions with divine-origin magic, which produces a different resonance pattern than standard arcane interruption. Fireball Evocation Adept Wizard · Sorcerer 8d6 fire damage in a 20-foot radius sphere. The most recognisable combat spell in Aethrya's magical tradition, and the one most reliably productive of collateral damage, witness reports, and investigative interest. Hub city use is restricted. Frontier deployment carries consequence risk proportional to what occupied the blast radius at the moment of detonation. Dispel Magic Abjuration Adept All Full Casters End magical effects on a target creature, object, or point in space. Cannot affect divine disguises, which are sustained by divine will rather than Weave-mediated magic. This limitation is documented in the Silent Synod's research as one of the primary empirical distinctions between divine and arcane magical effects — a distinction that most theological institutions have conspicuously declined to incorporate into their formal curricula. Hypnotic Pattern Illusion Adept Wizard · Sorcerer · Bard · Warlock A twisting pattern of colours fills a 30-foot cube, incapacitating creatures who fail their Wisdom save. Subject to Charm and Dominate Rules consent framework for player targets. One of the spells the Shar research division has documented as used by Darkness cell operatives during what the cells describe as "grief processing sessions." The documentation is not complete. Haste Transmutation Adept Wizard · Sorcerer One willing creature doubles in speed, gains +2 AC, advantage on Dexterity saves, and an additional action each turn. Concentration, one minute. The most significant single-target combat enhancement available at Adept tier — and the spell whose concentration loss at the wrong moment produces the most dramatically unfortunate results of any spell on the server. Spirit Guardians Conjuration Adept Cleric Summon spectral guardians that slow and damage enemies within 15 feet. Concentration. The primary reason Clerics are effective in extended combat situations — continuous area denial and damage that compounds with every round maintained. Doomguide Order combat doctrine is built around this spell at the Adept tier, and Casker Morvaine's coroner work means he encounters applications of it he did not expect to see. Leomund's Tiny Hut Evocation Adept (Ritual) Wizard · Bard Create a dome of magical force for 8 hours that protects occupants from the elements and prevents entry by creatures outside. Ritual casting only. The spell that makes wilderness travel genuinely survivable for caster-heavy groups — and the spell that creates a legally interesting question about whether a conversation conducted inside a Tiny Hut is admissible as evidence in Aldrus Cole's court, a question that has not yet been formally tested.

title: Expert Tier Spells
label: Spells & Rituals
excerpt: Fifth and sixth level spells — where the scale of magical possibility begins reshaping the world itself rather than simply affecting individuals within it. At Expert tier, a caster's choices carry consequences that extend well beyond the immediate scene, and the divine attention those choices attract is proportionally more focused.
text: Spells & Rituals Expert Tier Spells Fifth and sixth level spells — where the scale of magical possibility begins reshaping the world itself rather than simply affecting individuals within it. At Expert tier, a caster's choices carry consequences that extend well beyond the immediate scene, and the divine attention those choices attract is proportionally more focused. Hold Monster Enchantment Expert Wizard · Sorcerer · Warlock · Bard Paralyse any creature — including those immune to Charm — on a failed Wisdom save. Concentration. The Expert-tier escalation of Enchantment's controlling tradition: where Charm Person requires consent to apply narrative effects, Hold Monster's physical paralysis is a mechanical state with immediate observable consequences that cannot be declined. The distinction matters significantly in how it interacts with the Charm and Dominate Rules. Teleportation Circle Conjuration Expert (Ritual) Wizard · Bard · Sorcerer Inscribe a portal that connects to any permanent Teleportation Circle whose sigil sequence you know. The spell that transforms the server's geographic scale — Expert Wizards who know multiple sigil sequences can move between Leilon, Emberreach, and the Irondeep in ways that rewrite the logistics of faction operations. Every faction has either secured a permanent Circle or is actively seeking one. Tessara Wand knows the location and sigil of every registered Circle in the Throne of Ashes. Scrying Divination Expert Wizard · Druid · Cleric · Bard Observe a creature you are familiar with, regardless of distance, through an invisible sensor. The intelligence capability that makes Expert-tier diviners extraordinarily valuable and extraordinarily watched. The Veilbreakers have documented three attempts to Scry on suspected divine mortal-form agents. All three produced a result consistent with "lead-lined scrying resistance" — which is not a standard magical phenomenon. The files note this without drawing a conclusion. True Seeing Divination Expert Cleric · Wizard A creature you touch gains truesight, perceives illusions as false, and sees the Ethereal Plane for one hour. Partial interaction with divine disguises: True Seeing penetrates Weave-based illusion, but divine disguises sustained by divine will rather than the Weave may resist it entirely or produce only partial transparency. The distinction between resistance and partial transparency has produced three separate research papers from the Silent Synod, none of which agree on what the difference means. Wall of Force Evocation Expert Wizard An invisible wall of force springs into existence at a point you choose within range — immune to physical damage, immune to most magical dispelling, and persistent for ten minutes of concentration. The definitive containment spell at Expert tier: nothing passes through a Wall of Force except specific high-level magic. In a server where information control is as significant as physical control, the ability to isolate a conversation, a room, or a person from external interference for ten minutes is among the most valuable Expert-tier capabilities available.

title: Master Tier Spells
label: Spells & Rituals
excerpt: Seventh, eighth, and ninth level spells — magic at the scale of legend, where each casting reshapes reality in ways the world cannot ignore and divine networks cannot afford to overlook. Master-tier spellcasting in Aethrya is not merely powerful. It is geopolitically significant.
text: Spells & Rituals Master Tier Spells Seventh, eighth, and ninth level spells — magic at the scale of legend, where each casting reshapes reality in ways the world cannot ignore and divine networks cannot afford to overlook. Master-tier spellcasting in Aethrya is not merely powerful. It is geopolitically significant. Master-tier spell use is among the most observed magical activities in Aethrya. Mystra's Church registers every known caster who demonstrates this level of Weave access. Every divine faction that monitors magical activity has a protocol for when a mortal reaches this tier. The Judicators have a standing interest in any Master-tier casting that intersects with divine-adjacent affairs. You are, at this level, impossible to ignore — and the wise Master caster knows that being impossible to ignore is both a tremendous asset and a potentially terminal condition. Simulacrum Illusion Master Wizard Create a duplicate of a creature from snow or ice, endowed with half their HP and capabilities. The duplicate has no independent will and obeys the caster's commands. In Aethrya, a Simulacrum of a divine mortal-form agent is the most legally and theologically complex object any caster could produce — and several divine factions have explicitly instructed their mortal networks to report any Simulacrum casting immediately to their chain of command, without explanation. Wish Conjuration Master Wizard · Sorcerer The most powerful spell available to mortal spellcasters — capable of replicating any spell of 8th level or lower, or attempting effects beyond normal magical scope at the DM's determination. In Aethrya, Wish is managed as a DM event: the caster states their wish, and the DM determines its implementation in the context of the world's current narrative state. The wish-granting tradition's relationship to the Veil Accord is a matter of ongoing divine legal dispute that has never been formally resolved. Meteor Swarm Evocation Master Wizard · Sorcerer Blazing orbs of fire plummet from the sky at four points you choose within one mile — 40d6 fire and bludgeoning damage each, 40-foot radius. The most destructive spell available to mortal casters. Its use constitutes a hub city violation at any tier. Its use in populated territory is a Consequence Rules escalation that the DM team manages as a world event. Two historical uses have left geological features that are still visible in the landscape. The casters' names are still in the record. Foresight Divination Master Wizard · Druid · Bard · Warlock For 8 hours, one creature you touch cannot be surprised, has advantage on attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws, and all attacks against them have disadvantage. The definitive protective spell at Master tier — not through resistance but through awareness. Foresight is the spell that makes Master-tier diviners the most sought-after allies in any serious operation, and the one whose duration and passive nature makes it simultaneously the most valuable and least dramatic demonstration of what a Master diviner can provide. Astral Projection Necromancy Master Cleric · Monk · Warlock · Wizard Project into the Astral Plane with up to eight willing creatures, leaving your physical body behind. In Aethrya, the Astral Plane is not neutral ground — it is the corridor through which divine communication between the hidden gods and their true divine forms passes. A mortal who enters the Astral Plane at Master tier is entering a space where the Veil Accord's provisions become unclear and where what they might observe is something the Accord's authors did not intend mortals to see. DM consultation required before this spell is cast in a narrative context.

title: Rituals
label: Spells & Rituals
excerpt: The extended magical workings that require time rather than power — a different mode of spellcasting that trades immediacy for depth, and whose products often outlast any single combat encounter by days, weeks, or, in the case of certain divine-adjacent rituals, centuries.
text: Spells & Rituals Rituals The extended magical workings that require time rather than power — a different mode of spellcasting that trades immediacy for depth, and whose products often outlast any single combat encounter by days, weeks, or, in the case of certain divine-adjacent rituals, centuries. Ritual spells require 10 additional minutes of casting time but do not expend Spell Points when cast in this extended form. They represent careful, deliberate magical work rather than combat deployment — and in Aethrya, the distinction between careful deliberate magic and immediate force is often the distinction between a character who shapes the world and one who merely reacts to it. Divine rituals — those specific to religious practice and performed within institutional frameworks — carry additional layers of significance. When a Cleric of Kelemvor performs a death rite, Casker Morvaine is aware of it, in some sense, in a way that is difficult to categorise within standard magical taxonomy. When a Druid of Rillifane's network performs the Grove Renewal, Circle-Speaker Verdant's contamination map updates. Rituals in Aethrya are not merely magical procedures — they are the threads of a divine attention network that has been monitoring mortal magical practice for four thousand years. Notable Rituals by Institution Ritual Institution Effect Divine Principal Forge Blessing Morndinsamman forge-temples Enhances crafted item quality for 24 hours; maintains divine channel Moradin / Durn Anvilhand Death Rite Doomguide Order Directs a soul toward Kelemvor's judgement; prevents undead animation of the body Kelemvor / Casker Morvaine Grove Renewal Rillifane's Grove Network Updates contamination map; refreshes Grove Network communication links Rillifane / Circle-Speaker Verdant Moonmark Temple of the Moonmaiden Consecrates a path or location for safe travel; updates Reva Silverpath's route maps Selûne / Reva Silverpath Harvest Binding Harvest Fellowship Protects crops from blight and abnormal weather patterns for a growing season Chauntea / Maren Seedhallow Veilwatch Vigilant Order Places an invisible ward on a location that alerts the casting Cleric to intrusion Helm / Aldric Steadfast Darkness Rite Shar's Darkness Cells Creates a sanctified space for grief sharing; suppresses divination within Shar / Velara Dusk Certain ritual workings are prohibited under the Veil Accord's secondary provisions — specifically those designed to pierce the divine disguise, compel divine revelation, or communicate directly with a deity without going through their institutional mortal network. These rituals exist. They are documented in the Silent Synod's archives. Their use constitutes one of the few acts that will reliably produce a Judicator response within 24 hours of completion. The Veilbreakers have attempted two of them. Both attempts produced the same result: the ritual completed, produced data, and the data was incomplete in the same specific way. The Synod's annotation on this pattern is three words: "the gap persists."

title: Profession: Blacksmith
label: Professions
excerpt: The forge tradition of Aethrya — shaped by four thousand years of Moradin's mortal presence in the Irondeep, and the profession that produces the weapons, armour, and tools upon which every other profession ultimately depends.
text: Professions Profession: Blacksmith The forge tradition of Aethrya — shaped by four thousand years of Moradin's mortal presence in the Irondeep, and the profession that produces the weapons, armour, and tools upon which every other profession ultimately depends. Blacksmith Crafting Weapons & Armor Irondeep Tradition Primary Skills: Smithing · Material Knowledge · Forge Technique Key Output: Weapons · Armour · Tools · Masterwork commissions Divine Connection: Moradin's forge-temple tradition · Flandal Steelskin's masterwork standard Blacksmithing in Aethrya carries the weight of the Irondeep's deepest cultural tradition. A Novice smith produces serviceable tools; a Master smith produces work that other professionals bring to adventurers as aspirational examples. The forge-temple blessing ceremony practiced by dwarven smiths is technically available to non-dwarven Blacksmiths — and the character who receives a forge-temple blessing from Durn Anvilhand will find that the quality improvement is difficult to attribute entirely to standard divine blessing. The Artificer class's Spell Storing Item and the Master Blacksmith's commissioned masterwork interact in ways that make their collaboration the most sought-after professional partnership on the server.

title: Profession: Alchemist
label: Professions
excerpt: The practitioners who understand that magic and chemistry share a border that the Weave acknowledges without fully explaining — and whose outputs sustain the adventuring economy of an entire server.
text: Professions Profession: Alchemist The practitioners who understand that magic and chemistry share a border that the Weave acknowledges without fully explaining — and whose outputs sustain the adventuring economy of an entire server. Alchemist Crafting Consumables Research Adjacent Primary Skills: Alchemy · Herbalism · Chemical Analysis Key Output: Potions · Poisons · Reagents · Experimental compounds Divine Connection: Chauntea's herbalist tradition · Mystra's arcane research supply chain Alchemists supply the server's most consumed commodity — healing potions, buff consumables, and the reagents that spellcasting professions require in quantity. At Master tier, an Alchemist's Experimental Compound capability allows the development of novel substances with effects that have not been categorised in the standard pharmacopoeia — a research space the Imperial College monitors closely, for reasons that are not made fully explicit in their monitoring documentation.

title: Profession: Herbalist
label: Professions
excerpt: The botanical practitioners whose field knowledge outpaces any library — and whose work directly supports both the wound recovery system and the healing supply chains of every faction operating in the wilderness.
text: Professions Profession: Herbalist The botanical practitioners whose field knowledge outpaces any library — and whose work directly supports both the wound recovery system and the healing supply chains of every faction operating in the wilderness. Herbalist Gathering Medicine Grove-Adjacent Primary Skills: Nature · Medicine · Survival Key Output: Medicinal compounds · Wound treatment materials · Rare reagents Divine Connection: Chauntea's Harvest Fellowship · Rillifane's Grove Network Herbalists have a unique interaction with the Wound System — their treatments reduce wound recovery time for Moderate wounds by up to half and provide the standard treatment component required for Severe wound recovery. At Expert mastery, a Herbalist's botanical knowledge extends into the Grove Network's contamination tracking: unusual plant behaviour near Primordial Titan sites produces specimen characteristics that a trained Herbalist eye can identify and report to Circle-Speaker Verdant. Several of the Grove Network's contamination data points were initially flagged by Herbalist characters before the Druids confirmed them.

title: Profession: Cook
label: Professions
excerpt: The profession that sustains the bodies that sustain the world — whose outputs benefit every other character on the server, whose social role makes them one of the most naturally connected characters in any community, and whose relationship to Chauntea's domain is the most intimate of any non-divine profession.
text: Professions Profession: Cook The profession that sustains the bodies that sustain the world — whose outputs benefit every other character on the server, whose social role makes them one of the most naturally connected characters in any community, and whose relationship to Chauntea's domain is the most intimate of any non-divine profession. Cook Sustaining Profession Community Role Harvest Fellowship Adjacent Primary Skills: Cook's Utensils · Herbalism Kit · Persuasion Key Output: Meals · Long Rest benefits · Morale bonuses · Community gathering support Divine Connection: Chauntea's Harvest Fellowship · Yondalla's community tradition Cooks provide mechanical benefits through prepared meals that extend their effects into the subsequent Long Rest — temporary HP, stabilised wound recovery, enhanced morale. At Master tier, a Cook's feast produces effects that no other profession can replicate through their output alone. The social dimension of cooking — the gathering of people around a shared table — makes Cooks among the most naturally relationship-building characters in any settlement, and Chauntea's connection to the sustaining traditions of mortal life means that Maren Seedhallow has personal interest in the professional development of Master-tier Cooks. It is not unheard of for Maren to appear at the right moment with cultivation advice that improves an ingredient's quality in inexplicable ways.

title: Profession: Scholar
label: Professions
excerpt: The researchers, historians, and archivists whose professional domain is knowledge itself — and whose work, in a world where collective memory has been managed by hidden gods for four thousand years, is simultaneously the most dangerous and the most necessary profession available.
text: Professions Profession: Scholar The researchers, historians, and archivists whose professional domain is knowledge itself — and whose work, in a world where collective memory has been managed by hidden gods for four thousand years, is simultaneously the most dangerous and the most necessary profession available. Scholar Research Lore-Facing Veilbreaker Adjacent Primary Skills: History · Arcana · Investigation · Religion Key Output: Research papers · Decoded texts · Historical analyses · Identified inconsistencies Divine Connection: Mystra's Imperial College · Silent Synod adjacent · Corellon's Preservation Circles Scholars are the profession most naturally positioned for the Veilbreakers' Research Division, the Silent Synod's observation network, and the Preservation Circles' ongoing documentation work. Their mastery path advances from cataloguing known knowledge at Novice to producing original research that meaningfully advances understanding at Master — and in Aethrya, "meaningfully advances understanding" may mean identifying an inconsistency in the historical record that has been deliberately maintained by divine actors for centuries. The doctoral student who is currently under three divine agents' simultaneous monitoring is a Scholar. The research that placed them under that monitoring began as a standard academic paper.

title: Profession: Merchant
label: Professions
excerpt: From stall to empire — the Merchant's mastery path is Aethrya's most direct route from individual enterprise to cross-faction economic diplomacy, and the profession most closely watched by Vergadain's trade network.
text: Professions Profession: Merchant From stall to empire — the Merchant's mastery path is Aethrya's most direct route from individual enterprise to cross-faction economic diplomacy, and the profession most closely watched by Vergadain's trade network. Merchant Trade Information Economy Cross-Faction Primary Skills: Persuasion · Insight · Economics · Appraisal Key Output: Trade income · Faction trade contracts · Market intelligence Divine Connection: Vergadain's Bright Bargain network · Tymora's House of Many Chances At Novice, a Merchant operates a stall. At Adept, a permanent shop. At Expert, multi-location commercial operations and faction trade contracts. At Master, their accumulated relationship networks and commercial standing allow direct cross-faction economic negotiation — a capability that, in Aethrya's divine political landscape, places them at the intersection of every major power's economic interests simultaneously. Kessa Brightfall is known to favour merchants who take bold positions. The House of Many Chances occasionally finances exceptional commercial ventures. Both connections are noted in the Merchant's master-tier advancement documentation.

title: Profession: Hunter
label: Professions
excerpt: The professional harvesters of the wilderness — whose mastery of tracking, trapping, and territory management positions them as the frontier's most reliable economic operators and its most valuable source of intelligence about what is actually happening in the unmapped Veillands.
text: Professions Profession: Hunter The professional harvesters of the wilderness — whose mastery of tracking, trapping, and territory management positions them as the frontier's most reliable economic operators and its most valuable source of intelligence about what is actually happening in the unmapped Veillands. Hunter Gathering Frontier Economy Tracking Primary Skills: Survival · Stealth · Animal Handling · Nature Key Output: Hides · Meat · Bones · Rare creature components Divine Connection: Malar's beast tradition (contested) · Chauntea's rural network Hunters are the frontier's primary early-warning system for unusual creature behaviour — and in the current season, unusual creature behaviour near Primordial Titan manifestation sites is one of the most significant intelligence streams available. A Hunter who documents coordinated animal territorial withdrawal from specific regions, brings specimens of animals behaving abnormally to the Grove Network, or maps the boundary of a contamination zone through creature movement patterns is doing investigative work that no other profession is positioned to perform. The season's Titan awakening arc runs partly through the Hunter profession's field operations.

title: Help & FAQ
label: Support
excerpt: Answers to the most common questions about Aethrya — from initial server access to advanced mechanics. If your question isn't answered here, every channel on this page connects you to the team member who can help.
text: Support Help & FAQ Answers to the most common questions about Aethrya — from initial server access to advanced mechanics. If your question isn't answered here, every channel on this page connects you to the team member who can help. How do I join Aethrya? ▶ Join our Discord server through the invite link in our community channels. Complete the whitelist application form pinned in #whitelist-applications. Create your character concept (race, class, profession, brief backstory) and submit through the application form. Staff review typically completes within 48–72 hours. Upon approval you receive server access and your getting-started package. Can I play a Fabled race immediately? ▶ Fabled race applications require an established record on the server first. The standard path is: complete the whitelist with a Common race character, demonstrate roleplay quality over a minimum of 2 active weeks, then submit a Fabled application. The Lore Team reviews the full application and conducts an interview before approval. Exceptions to the established-record requirement are rare and require extraordinary application quality. How does the mastery system work in practice? ▶ You begin at Novice. Advancing to Adept requires attending three DM sessions, demonstrating your character's personality and factional role consistently, and receiving DM team confirmation. Post the advancement request in #mastery-advancement with links to relevant scene logs. The DM team typically responds within one week. Expert and Master follow the same process with elevated requirements — see the Mastery Progression page for full details. What if I disagree with a DM ruling? ▶ DM rulings during active scenes are final in the moment — do not contest them in scene. After the scene concludes, submit a question or concern through #mod-mail or a private staff ticket. Senior staff review disputed rulings within 72 hours. Rulings confirmed as errors are corrected retroactively where possible. The appeal process exists for genuine errors, not for outcomes you disliked. How does PvP work? ▶ Player-versus-player combat requires: established IC motivation, notification to the DM team before the conflict begins, OOC acknowledgment from both parties that PvP is occurring, and compliance with the consent framework. Unprovoked ambush-style PvP requires DM pre-approval. PvP death is permanent only with DM approval. The Combat Rules page covers the full PvP protocol. What's the difference between arcane and divine magic? ▶ Arcane magic flows through Mystra's Weave — the living divine framework she maintains. It is subject to Weave conditions, requires Church of the Seven Stars registration, and cannot create divine disguises because it is Weave-dependent. Divine magic flows through a direct patron connection — it is not Weave-dependent, but the deity on the other end is always aware of its use. Cantrips, Novice, Adept, Expert, and Master tiers apply to both systems. The Magic & The Weave page covers the full system. How do I find other players to roleplay with? ▶ Check #scene-seeking in Discord — players post available scene requests and open scene announcements there. The hub cities (Leilon, Phandalin) have dedicated public space channels where drop-in scenes are always available. DM events are the most reliable way to meet other players across factional lines. If you're new and struggling to find scenes, post in #new-player-help and the community team will connect you with active players in your area of interest.

title: Feedback & Suggestions
label: Support
excerpt: Aethrya is a living collaborative creation — the DM team's frameworks, the lore team's world, and the community's evolving story are all shaped by what you experience as a player. Your perspective is not incidental; it is part of the process by which the server improves.
text: Support Feedback & Suggestions Aethrya is a living collaborative creation — the DM team's frameworks, the lore team's world, and the community's evolving story are all shaped by what you experience as a player. Your perspective is not incidental; it is part of the process by which the server improves. We actively seek feedback on all dimensions of the Aethrya experience: rules clarity, lore consistency, event pacing, faction balance, profession economy, NPC encounter quality, and anything else that shapes your experience of the world. You cannot break the server by giving honest feedback. You can make it better. Feedback Channels 📋 #general-feedback Open-channel feedback on any aspect of the server. Public and visible to the full staff team. The best channel for suggestions you'd like community discussion around. 🎲 #rules-questions Rules clarification requests and edge case questions. Staff responses here are treated as official rulings for the scenario described. The channel's history functions as Aethrya's living FAQ. 🌍 #lore-suggestions World-building suggestions, lore questions, and proposals for how your character's arc could connect to the server's larger story. The Lore Team reads every submission in this channel. 🔒 Staff Ticket Private feedback on sensitive matters — player conduct concerns, personal safety issues, or feedback about a specific staff member's handling of a situation. All tickets are reviewed by senior staff. What We Do With Feedback Every substantive piece of feedback enters a review process. Rules suggestions are discussed in weekly staff meetings. Lore suggestions are evaluated against the established world framework and, when compatible and compelling, integrated into the next appropriate story beat. Player conduct feedback is actioned through the moderation pipeline with appropriate confidentiality. You will receive a response to every ticket, typically within 48 hours. The server's most significant improvements — the wound system's recovery curve adjustment, the Fabled application process redesign, the Charm Rules consent framework expansion — all began as player feedback submissions. The world you inhabit tomorrow is being shaped by what you share with us today.

title: Profession: Quartermaster
label: Professions
excerpt: The logistical backbone of factions, settlements, and adventuring companies — the profession of volume, management, and the economic leverage that comes from controlling what flows where.
text: Professions Profession: Quartermaster The logistical backbone of factions, settlements, and adventuring companies — the profession of volume, management, and the economic leverage that comes from controlling what flows where. Quartermaster Logistics Faction-Facing Trade Economy Primary Skills: Logistics · Negotiation · Inventory Management Key Output: Bulk goods · Faction supply contracts · Masterwork commissioning Divine Connection: Vergadain's trade network · Chauntea's Harvest Fellowship supply chain Quartermasters are the lifeblood of organised operations in Aethrya. Their mastery path runs from managing personal inventory at Novice, through negotiating standing agreements with faction supply officers at Adept, to directing faction-wide supply operations at Master that constitute genuine economic leverage — the ability to withhold or redirect supply creates political pressure that no amount of martial capability can easily counter. Goldan Brightpurse's travelling merchant operation includes a Quartermaster function that serves as Vergadain's primary economic intelligence tool across the Irondeep's cross-race trade relationships. Mastery Output Scale Faction Access Novice Personal inventory management; basic supply runs Local trader standing Adept Group supply management; standard contracts Minor faction supply officer contact Expert Settlement supply operations; bulk contract negotiation Major faction logistics partnership Master Faction-wide supply direction; cross-faction economic leverage Direct access to all major faction economic channels

text: Professions Profession: Miner The practitioners who know what the mountain keeps — and whose professional intimacy with geological reality makes them, in the current season, among the first mortals to notice that the stone is doing something it has not done before. Miner Gathering Resource Extraction Irondeep Adjacent Primary Skills: Athletics · History (geological) · Tool Use Key Output: Ore · Gemstones · Rare minerals · Geological reports Divine Connection: Dumathoin's deep-place tradition · Moradin's forge supply chain Miners in Phandalin's Wave Echo Cave system have been first-hand witnesses to the Primordial Titan awakening's most concrete physical expressions — the collapsed shafts, the stone that moved in non-geological ways, the subsonic vibration patterns that veteran miners describe as unfamiliar. A Miner character who works the deeper sections of any active mine near a Titan manifestation site and documents what they encounter is producing primary source material that the Grove Network, the Silent Synod, and at least two divine mortal-form agents will find significant. Dumathoin's domain includes what the stone keeps. His mortal-form network is paying attention to what it releases.

Scholar Research Lore-Facing Veilbreaker Adjacent Primary Skills: History · Arcana · Investigation · Religion Key Output: Research papers · Decoded texts · Historical analyses · Identified inconsistencies Divine Connection: Mystra's Imperial College · Silent Synod adjacent · Corellon's Preservation Circles Scholars are the profession most naturally positioned for the Veilbreakers' Research Division, the Silent Synod's observation network, and the Preservation Circles' ongoing documentation work. Their mastery path advances from cataloguing known knowledge at Novice to producing original research that meaningfully advances understanding at Master — and in Aethrya, "meaningfully advances understanding" may mean identifying an inconsistency in the historical record that has been deliberately maintained by divine actors for centuries. The doctoral student who is currently under three divine agents' simultaneous monitoring is a Scholar. The research that placed them under that monitoring began as a standard academic paper.

Cook Sustaining Profession Community Role Harvest Fellowship Adjacent Primary Skills: Cook's Utensils · Herbalism Kit · Persuasion Key Output: Meals · Long Rest benefits · Morale bonuses · Community gathering support Divine Connection: Chauntea's Harvest Fellowship · Yondalla's community tradition Cooks provide mechanical benefits through prepared meals that extend their effects into the subsequent Long Rest — temporary HP, stabilised wound recovery, enhanced morale. At Master tier, a Cook's feast produces effects that no other profession can replicate through their output alone. The social dimension of cooking — the gathering of people around a shared table — makes Cooks among the most naturally relationship-building characters in any settlement, and Chauntea's connection to the sustaining traditions of mortal life means that Maren Seedhallow has personal interest in the professional development of Master-tier Cooks. It is not unheard of for Maren to appear at the right moment with cultivation advice that improves an ingredient's quality in inexplicable ways.

text: Spells & Rituals Cantrips & Fluff Magic Zero-cost magical expressions available to all spellcasters — the ambient magic of daily life in Aethrya. In a world where every arcane cast is drawn through Mystra's living Weave, even the smallest thread is observed. Nothing is truly incidental. Cantrips cost 0 SP and are available to all spellcasters at any mastery tier. They represent the most practised, most automatic expressions of a spellcaster's connection to their magical source — muscle memory translated into magic. In Aethrya, cantrips have a particular cultural role: they are the visible layer of magical ability that non-magical characters observe, and their presence or absence in a character's daily behaviour is often the first indicator to observant NPCs that magical capability is present at all. Fluff Magic encompasses non-combat utility cantrips — small magical expressions that serve narrative and atmospheric rather than mechanical purposes. Characters may use fluff magic freely without tracking costs. It enriches scenes, establishes magical presence, and creates the texture of a world where magic is woven into ordinary life. Common Cantrips Cantrip School Available To Effect Prestidigitation Transmutation Wizard, Bard, Sorcerer Minor magical effects — clean, soil, colour, flavour, small sounds, small illusions. The most versatile utility cantrip. Thaumaturgy Transmutation Cleric Manifest minor divine signs — booming voice, shaking ground, flaming eyes, opened doors. Social presence at religious events. Druidcraft Transmutation Druid Predict weather, bloom a flower, create a leaf-whisper, light/snuff a flame. The Grove Network's ambient presence in natural spaces. Eldritch Blast Evocation Warlock 1d10 force damage per beam — the Warlock's primary combat cantrip, enhanced by Agonizing Blast and other Invocations. Sacred Flame Evocation Cleric Radiant flame descends — ignores cover. The most visible expression of divine affiliation in daily use, which is diplomatically significant in Southkrypt. Firebolt Evocation Wizard, Sorcerer, Artificer 1d10 fire damage at range — reliable arcane combat cantrip. Can ignite flammable objects, which in enclosed spaces is consequential. Mage Hand Conjuration Wizard, Bard, Sorcerer, Warlock Invisible spectral hand for manipulation at a distance. The reconnaissance cantrip — useful in spaces you cannot safely enter with your body. Minor Illusion Illusion Wizard, Bard, Sorcerer, Warlock, Gnomes Create a sound or image within 30 feet. The cantrip that has appeared in more Veilbreaker incident reports than any other single spell. Guidance Divination Cleric, Druid +1d4 to one ability check. The most used support cantrip on the server, cast on virtually every significant skill check. Chauntea's Harvest Fellowship Clerics cast it reflexively on almost every ally action. Toll the Dead Necromancy Cleric, Warlock, Wizard Necrotic damage — heightened against injured targets. The Doomguide Order's combat cantrip of choice. In the hands of Casker Morvaine's staff, its use in a hub city constitutes an extremely pointed statement. Every arcane cantrip cast in the Throne of Ashes is, in a technical sense, observed by Mystra's ongoing Weave monitoring. She does not actively attend every Prestidigitation cast in every tavern. But the Church of the Seven Stars' arcane census captures patterns across all registered casters — frequency of use, spell school preference, casting hour — and these patterns are reviewed by Tessara Wand's network analysts weekly. The doctoral student's cantrip casting pattern was flagged fourteen months ago. It took this long for the monitoring recommendation to reach the relevant divine agents.
